p.strong: Side-by-side comparison of blocking vs yielding behavior in Coroutine Runtimes

div.p: In M:N scheduling (M tasks on N threads), blocking a thread via std::sync::Mutex creates a Head-of-Line Blocking issue where one slow I/O or contended lock freezes unrelated tasks. AsyncMutex integrates with the runtime's reactor/executor to ensure the thread is repurposed immediately upon a lock conflict.

ul.li: Side Effect: Stop-the-world for all tasks on this thread.
Efficiency: Low. OS doesn't know about coroutines.
Side Effect: Thread A remains 100% utilized.
